description_long: This object consists of a loosely clustered group of tall, thin, grass-like shrubs with sparse, upright stems and fine, wispy foliage. The stems are mostly vertical, with some slight outward splaying, and the tufts are distributed irregularly across a patch of ground. The foliage appears dry and straw-colored, with a mix of light brown and grayish tones, giving the impression of hardy, drought-tolerant vegetation. The base of each shrub is denser, while the upper portions are more open and airy, allowing visibility through the clumps. There are no visible flowers or fruits, and the overall structure is open, with significant gaps between individual tufts. This type of shrub is commonly found in urban landscaping, roadside verges, or low-maintenance public spaces.
description: A group of tall, wispy, grass-like shrubs with sparse, upright stems and dry, straw-colored foliage, arranged in an irregular cluster. The tufts are open and airy, with no visible flowers or fruits.
description_view_0: From this angle, the irregular spacing and upright, wispy stems of the shrubs are clearly visible, with denser bases and open tops.
description_view_1: This view highlights the depth and staggered arrangement of the shrub clumps, showing more of the gaps between tufts.
description_view_2: The open, airy structure of the shrubs is most apparent here, with the vertical stems and sparse foliage allowing visibility through the cluster.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the linear distribution and the varying heights of the shrub tufts, with the dry, straw-like texture prominent.
